blocks.text: Holy ETH! A sandstorm, and then this dark cave! I never thought I'd say this, but I'm so grateful to the voices in my head. If it weren't for them I'd be buried in there forever and ever. My time to die is yet to come, and I will do my best to survive and help my friends get to the promised land safe and sound.

If I could choose between being buried alive in a cave and suffocating in a sandstorm, then I would definitely choose the latter. A lot of my brothers disappeared in the mines, finding resources for the rich. I  have not been through so much only to die under the stony rubble like they did. This cave will not be my grave. And another thing, my eyes are naturally protected from wind and dust, so death in a sandstorm would not be so bad.

I am so jealous! Why did it have to be Rad who found the little magic golden protective thing? I need it. Oh well, I can protect myself, and I am going to. But it's so shiny. And, most importantly, it is not MINE! I can't get this wonderful, pretty thing out of my head. I can't wait to nick it from Rad, I just need to wait for the perfect moment...

Look at our bodies, how weak they are. Our spirits are broken and our fate is unknown. We seek salvation in a cave, just like wild animals, and as soon as we hear the clatter of stones, we run away. We are pitiful and insignificant. I will teach you how to subdue the fear within yourself, for fear is the destiny of beasts. I will teach you how to defeat your body, which commits you to die. To overcome the body means to overcome death. I will teach you the power that can conquer weakness and the weak. We were born to live in this Universe, not to flee from it. You just need to step over the abyss of compassion.

Many heroes hide their faces with a mask so that their affairs become more important than their personality. I hide under a hood, and I must say that a mask is much more practical in the sand storm. But in the sun and in the rain a hood is more convenient. Take, for example, a cloak, like the one Black Hat wears. Such a useless thing! It can be pinned to the ground by a stone, or it can be grabbed by the enemy in battle. A hood is another matter. It protects from bad weather, does not constrain movement during the battle, can easily be repaired, hides weapons and disguises the hero. So many advantages! You may consider this tedious, but heroism is a dangerous business: you have to be prepared for it.

Hmm. Do I have to say something? I'm so tired of all this... I'm alive, but others have died. I don't know where we're going anymore, and why? Why do some have to die while others survive? What's the point? I do not know anything anymore... I just don't understand. Maybe we'll wake up tomorrow and it'll all be just a bad dream. I would like that to be so. Yes, perhaps, I'd like to start over. It's a pity it's impossible. Excuse me... I'm... I'm sorry.

Let's stop for a second and look back. What are we doing? We seek refuge, only to perish. We are the last pieces of civilization, and we give our lives for nothing. But why does this happen? Who brought us here? Wise Nao? Where is he? Maybe he is the source of all our problems. Let's compare the facts. The farther he leads us to salvation, the fewer tokens are left to be saved. This is something to think about. We must always hold accountable those who make decisions for the whole community. He must bear responsibility for his mistakes.
